generations.user_prompt: Let's prepare for implementing phase 1 using the cosa_phase1_spect.md file

generated_stories.title: Project Bootstrap and Configuration Loading
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want COSA to load and validate configuration from appliance.yaml and .env on startup so that the agent has all required parameters before starting any services.
acceptance_criteria: main.js reads appliance.yaml and .env on startup
All required fields (SSH config, email credentials, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, operator email) are validated at startup
Process exits with a descriptive error if any required field is missing
Config is exposed as a singleton accessible by all modules
Database migrations run automatically after config is loaded

generated_stories.title: session.db Schema and Migration
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the SQLite session database to be created and migrated automatically so that all agent activity can be persisted from the first run.
acceptance_criteria: session.db is created at data/session.db if it does not exist
WAL mode is enabled on the database
All five tables are created: sessions, turns, tool_calls, approvals, alerts
FTS5 virtual table turns_fts is created and linked to turns
All indexes defined in the spec are created
Migrations are idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

generated_stories.title: Session Store CRUD Operations
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a session-store module that encapsulates all reads and writes to session.db so that other modules never interact with the database directly.
acceptance_criteria: createSession(sessionId, trigger) inserts a row into sessions
saveTurn(sessionId, role, content, tokensIn, tokensOut) inserts a row into turns
saveToolCall(sessionId, toolCall, result, status) inserts a row into tool_calls
recordBlockedToolCall(sessionId, toolCall, reason) inserts a blocked tool_calls row
createApproval(approvalData) inserts a row into approvals
findApprovalByToken(token) returns the matching approval row
updateApprovalStatus(approvalId, status, resolvedBy, note) updates the approval row
closeSession(sessionId, summary) sets status=complete and summary on the session row
All writes use parameterized queries (no SQL injection risk)

generated_stories.title: SSH Backend with Connection Pool and Reconnect
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want an SSH backend module that maintains a persistent connection to the Baanbaan Pi and reconnects automatically on failure so that tools can reliably execute remote commands.
acceptance_criteria: exec(command) runs a shell command on the Baanbaan Pi and returns { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
isConnected() returns the current connection state
Reconnect attempts use exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s
Command timeout is enforced using the value from appliance.yaml (default 30s)
Connection parameters (host, port, user, key_path) are read from appliance.yaml
command argument is never constructed from user input or LLM output — enforced by module contract
SSH connectivity is tested at startup; failure is logged as a warning but does not crash the process

generated_stories.title: Security Gate — Dangerous Command Detection and Output Sanitization
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a security gate module that blocks dangerous tool calls and strips credentials from tool output so that destructive or sensitive operations never reach the appliance or the LLM conversation.
acceptance_criteria: check(toolCall) tests the stringified tool input against all patterns in appliance.yaml security.dangerous_commands
Returns { blocked: true, reason, pattern } when a pattern matches
Returns { blocked: false } when no pattern matches
Pattern matching is case-insensitive
sanitizeOutput(output) replaces Anthropic API keys, generic secrets, password=, and token= patterns with [REDACTED]
Each dangerous command pattern has both a positive test case (should block) and a negative test case (should not block)
100% unit test coverage

generated_stories.title: Tool Registry — Registration, Schema Validation, and Dispatch
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a tool registry that registers tools by name, validates inputs against their JSON schema, and dispatches calls to the correct handler so that the orchestrator has a single, consistent interface for all tool invocations.
acceptance_criteria: register(name, schema, handler) adds a tool to the registry
getSchemas() returns tool definitions in the Anthropic API tool_use format
dispatch(name, input) validates input against the tool's JSON schema before calling the handler
dispatch throws a structured error if the tool name is unknown
dispatch throws a structured error if input fails schema validation
Only tools with enabled: true in appliance.yaml are registered and returned by getSchemas()
90% unit test coverage

generated_stories.title: health_check Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to run a comprehensive health check against the Baanbaan appliance so that I can report its status to the operator or trigger an alert.
acceptance_criteria: Performs SSH connectivity check first; all subsequent steps depend on it
Makes direct LAN HTTP GET to {appliance_api.base_url}/health with 10s timeout
Makes direct LAN HTTP GET to {appliance_api.base_url}/health/ready with 10s timeout
Runs systemctl show baanbaan --property=ActiveState,SubState,ExecMainStartTimestamp,NRestarts via SSH
Parses key=value output from systemctl and computes uptime_seconds from ExecMainStartTimestamp
Returns overall_status: 'healthy' only when SSH connected, both HTTP checks return 200 with ok status, and systemd unit is active/running with 0 restarts
Returns overall_status: 'degraded' for partial failures (non-ok HTTP body, recent restarts)
Returns overall_status: 'unreachable' when SSH fails or HTTP completely fails
Output matches the defined JSON schema
Risk level is 'read' — no approval required

generated_stories.title: db_query Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to execute read-only SQL SELECT queries against the Baanbaan SQLite database via SSH so that I can answer operational questions about orders, revenue, and application state.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts a query string and optional limit (default 50, max 100)
Rejects queries that do not start with SELECT
Rejects queries containing DROP, DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, CREATE, ALTER, or ATTACH
Appends LIMIT {limit} if not already present in the query
Executes via SSH: sqlite3 -json -readonly {database.path} "{query}"
Returns { rows, row_count, truncated, query_time_ms }
Sets truncated: true when row_count equals the limit
Risk level is 'read' — no approval required
Query timeout enforced per appliance.yaml (default 15s)

generated_stories.title: db_integrity Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to run SQLite integrity and WAL checkpoint checks on the Baanbaan database via SSH so that I can detect corruption early and report database health.
acceptance_criteria: Runs PRAGMA integrity_check via SSH on the Baanbaan database path
Sets is_healthy: true only when integrity result is exactly 'ok'
Runs PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE) when run_wal_checkpoint is true (default)
Parses WAL checkpoint output into { ran, busy, log, checkpointed }
Returns errors array for any failures during execution
Includes checked_at timestamp in output
Risk level is 'read' — no approval required

generated_stories.title: Approval Engine — Token Generation, FSM, and Expiry
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want an approval engine that generates secure tokens, tracks approval state, and enforces timeouts so that high-impact actions are never executed without operator consent.
acceptance_criteria: requiresApproval(toolCall) returns 'auto' for read-risk tools and 'once' for medium/high/critical
requestApproval(sessionId, toolCall, policy) creates an approval record in session.db and sends an approval request email
Token format is APPROVE-{8 uppercase hex chars} generated via crypto.randomBytes(4)
Tokens are single-use and unique
Default approval timeout is 30 minutes; urgent timeout is 5 minutes
processInboundReply(msg) handles APPROVE-TOKEN, DENY, and ambiguous replies
Approved token executes the waiting action and sends confirmation email
Denied approval logs the operator note and sends cancellation email
Background expiry check runs every 5 minutes; expired approvals trigger notification email
All FSM state transitions (pending → approved/denied/expired) are logged to session.db
100% unit test coverage for all FSM transitions

generated_stories.title: Email Gateway — IMAP Polling and SMTP Sending
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want an email gateway that polls Gmail for inbound messages and sends plain-text SMTP replies so that the operator can interact with COSA via email.
acceptance_criteria: IMAP polls the COSA inbox every 60 seconds
Only processes messages from the configured operator email address; others are silently ignored and logged
Detects approval replies (APPROVE-TOKEN or DENY pattern) and routes to approvalEngine.processInboundReply
Non-approval messages trigger a new orchestrator session
SMTP send supports to, subject, text, inReplyTo, and references fields
All outbound emails are plain text — no HTML
Email threading headers (In-Reply-To, References) are set correctly on replies
Processed messages are marked as READ in the IMAP inbox
Gmail App Password authentication used (IMAP host: imap.gmail.com:993, SMTP host: smtp.gmail.com:587)
80% unit test coverage with mocked IMAP/SMTP

generated_stories.title: Cron Scheduler — Health Check Task
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want a cron scheduler that fires a full orchestrator session on a defined schedule so that health checks run autonomously every hour without operator intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Registers the health-check task with cron expression 0 * * * * from appliance.yaml
Each cron tick invokes the orchestrator with the structured health check trigger message defined in §11.2
If overall_status is 'healthy', no email is sent and the result is logged to session.db
If overall_status is 'degraded' or 'unreachable', an alert email is sent to the operator
Alert deduplication: a new alert for the same category is suppressed if an alert was sent less than 60 minutes ago at the same severity
Alert is recorded in the alerts table with severity, category, title, body, sent_at, and email_to

generated_stories.title: Orchestrator Core Loop
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want a core orchestration loop that receives triggers, calls the Claude API, dispatches tool calls through the security and approval gates, and routes the final response back to the caller so that the full agent workflow runs end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: runSession(trigger) creates a session record, builds the system prompt, and calls the Claude API
Tool calls are dispatched through securityGate.check before execution
Blocked tool calls are logged and an error is returned to the LLM conversation
Tool calls at medium/high/critical risk are routed through approvalEngine before execution
Tool output is sanitized via securityGate.sanitizeOutput before being appended to conversation history
Loop terminates on stop_reason: end_turn and returns the final text response
Loop terminates after 20 iterations maximum and returns a graceful message to the caller
All turns and tool calls are logged to session.db
Session is closed with a summary on completion or error
Uses claude-sonnet-4-6 model for all Phase 1 sessions

generated_stories.title: Context Builder — System Prompt Assembly
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a context builder that assembles the layered system prompt for each session so that COSA always has the correct identity, appliance context, and tool schemas when calling the Claude API.
acceptance_criteria: build() returns a single string composed of: COSA identity, APPLIANCE.md content, filtered tool schemas, and current ISO timestamp
Sections are separated by a consistent delimiter (--- or similar)
APPLIANCE.md is read from config/APPLIANCE.md
Only tools with enabled: true in appliance.yaml are included in tool schemas
COSA identity text matches the specification in §12.2 exactly
build() is deterministic for the same config and tool registry state

generated_stories.title: Structured JSON Logging
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want all COSA modules to emit structured JSON logs to stdout so that operational issues can be diagnosed from log output.
acceptance_criteria: Every log line is valid JSON with ts, level, module, and msg fields
Log levels are debug, info, warn, error
Log level is configured via COSA_LOG_LEVEL in .env
All modules include their module name in log output
No plaintext log lines are emitted — all output is structured JSON

generated_stories.title: End-to-End Staging Test: All 8 Functional Scenarios
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want all 8 functional scenarios from the Phase 1 spec to pass in a staging environment so that Phase 1 exit criteria are verified before declaring the implementation complete.
acceptance_criteria: F-1: Operator email query returns accurate health status within 2 minutes
F-2: Cron health check on healthy appliance produces no outbound email and one session.db row
F-3: Cron health check on unreachable appliance sends alert email within 5 minutes
F-4: Cron health check on degraded appliance sends degraded alert email with specific warning details
F-5: Operator replies with APPROVE-TOKEN — approval status updated and confirmation email sent
F-6: Operator replies with DENY — approval status set to denied and cancellation email sent
F-7: No operator reply within timeout — approval status set to expired and notification email sent
F-8: Dangerous command (DROP TABLE) in tool input is blocked before execution and logged with status=blocked
All scenarios verified against a real or simulated Baanbaan Pi exposing the §18.1 health endpoints
NODE_ENV=staging uses 2-minute approval timeout

generations.user_prompt: Let's now prepare to implement phase 2

generated_stories.title: FTS5 Triggers for Session Turn Search
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want FTS5 INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on the turns table so that session_search returns up-to-date results without manual rebuilds.
acceptance_criteria: Three triggers exist: turns_fts_insert, turns_fts_delete, turns_fts_update
Inserting a turn via saveTurn() causes it to appear in turns_fts immediately
Updating a turn updates its FTS5 index entry
Deleting a turn removes its FTS5 index entry
Triggers are created idempotently in runMigrations()
searchTurns(query, limit) exported from session-store.js returns ranked results with session_id, turn_index, role, content, created_at

generated_stories.title: skills.db Schema and Migration
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the SQLite skills database created and migrated automatically so that the skill library is available from the first run.
acceptance_criteria: skills.db is created at data/skills.db if it does not exist
WAL mode is enabled
skills table is created with all columns: id, name, title, description, domain, content, version, use_count, last_used_at, created_at, updated_at
skills_fts FTS5 virtual table is created and linked to skills
skill_uses table is created with all columns
All defined indexes are created
Migrations are idempotent

generated_stories.title: Skill Store CRUD and Seed Install
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a skill-store module that installs seed skills on first run and exposes CRUD operations so that COSA has operational knowledge from day one.
acceptance_criteria: On first run, 8 seed skill markdown files from skills/seed/ are inserted into skills.db
Seed install is idempotent — does not duplicate skills on subsequent startups
list() returns all skills as an array
listCompact() returns a formatted string (~30 tokens/skill) for use in the system prompt
get(name) returns the full skill document or null if not found
create(skill) inserts a new skill and returns the inserted row
improve(name, experience) appends an experience entry to the skill's ## Experience section
improve() trims ## Experience to the 5 most recent entries if document exceeds 3000 chars

generated_stories.title: Memory Manager — MEMORY.md Load, Update, and Limit Enforcement
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a memory-manager module that loads MEMORY.md at session start and enforces a 2200-character hard limit so that COSA's persistent memory never grows unbounded.
acceptance_criteria: loadMemory() reads data/MEMORY.md and returns its contents; returns an empty template if the file does not exist
updateMemory(patch) merges patch fields into the existing MEMORY.md sections and writes back to disk
writeMemory(content) replaces MEMORY.md entirely after enforcing the character limit
If a write would exceed 2200 characters, the oldest Recent Incidents entry is pruned and the write is retried
If still over limit after pruning incidents, the Notes section is truncated to 100 characters
The 2200-character hard limit is never exceeded after any write
updateMemory() implemented as a SAM acceptor per Phase 2 §19.4

generated_stories.title: Context Builder — 10-Layer Prompt Assembly with Cache Hints
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the context builder updated to support the full 10-layer prompt architecture with Anthropic cache breakpoints so that Layers 0–4 cache on every session.
acceptance_criteria: build(options) accepts memory, skillIndex, activeSkills, and sessionSummary options
Output is an array of system blocks suitable for the Anthropic messages.create() system parameter
Layers 0–4 (identity, APPLIANCE.md, OPERATIONS.md, skill index, MEMORY.md) are concatenated into a single block with cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' }
Layers 5–9 are assembled fresh each call and returned as separate blocks
If memory is not provided, Layer 4 is omitted rather than empty
If skillIndex is not provided, Layer 3 is omitted
build() is deterministic for the same inputs

generated_stories.title: Context Compressor — Summarize Middle Turns
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a context-compressor module that detects when the message array is too long and summarizes the middle turns via Haiku so that long sessions never exceed context limits.
acceptance_criteria: needsCompression(messages) returns true when messages.length exceeds config.contextCompression.maxTurnsBeforeCompress
compress(messages, sessionId) protects the first 3 and last 4 turns
compress() calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to summarize the middle turns
The summary is injected as a single user message prefixed with '[Context summary — prior turns compressed]'
The returned array length equals protectFirstN + 1 + protectLastN = 8 (with default config)
A compression event is logged to session.db as a system-role turn
Compression is implemented as a SAM action/acceptor pair per Phase 2 §19.3
Acceptor rejects (no Haiku call) if needsCompression returns false

generated_stories.title: shift_report Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to aggregate the previous day's appliance records into a structured summary so that I can generate and send the daily shift report email.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts lookback_hours (default 24, max 48) and an optional date (YYYY-MM-DD) override
Executes a read-only db_query to aggregate min/max/avg temperature, avg humidity, total readings, and condition breakdown
Returns period_start, period_end, total_readings, temperature object, humidity object, conditions array, and anomalies array
Risk level is 'read'
Returns an error if no readings exist for the requested period rather than silently returning zeros

generated_stories.title: archive_search Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to search across historical session turns using FTS5 so that I can recall prior incident resolutions when handling repeat issues.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts query (required, FTS5 syntax) and limit (default 10, max 50)
Delegates to session-store.searchTurns()
Returns results array with session_id, turn_index, role, created_at, and excerpt (200 chars max)
Returns total_found count
Risk level is 'read'
off_hours_gate config key is parsed but inactive for WeatherStation

generated_stories.title: backup_run Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to trigger a backup of the appliance database so that data is preserved and can be verified afterward.
acceptance_criteria: Exports the readings table to a timestamped JSONL file in the configured backup directory
Writes a .sha256 sidecar file containing the SHA-256 checksum of the JSONL
Returns success, backup_path, row_count, checksum, started_at, completed_at, duration_ms
Risk level is 'medium'
Timeout is enforced per appliance.yaml (default 120s)
Returns success: false with an error message rather than throwing on SSH failure

generated_stories.title: backup_verify Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to verify the most recent backup's checksum so that I can detect corruption before it becomes an unrecoverable data loss.
acceptance_criteria: Reads the most recent backup file from the configured directory if backup_path is not specified
Re-computes the SHA-256 checksum of the JSONL file
Compares against the expected hash from the .sha256 sidecar
Returns verified, backup_path, expected_hash, actual_hash, row_count, file_size_kb, backup_age_hours
Returns verified: false (not a throw) when checksums do not match
Throws if no backup file is found at all
Risk level is 'read'

generated_stories.title: settings_write Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to update a single appliance configuration key-value so that operator-requested configuration changes can be applied safely.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts key, value (both required), and optional reason
Rejects keys not in the allowed_keys allowlist from appliance.yaml with a structured error
Reads the old value before writing
Executes parameterized INSERT OR REPLACE into the station_info table via SSH
Returns success, key, old_value, new_value, applied_at
Risk level is 'medium'

generated_stories.title: restart_appliance Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to issue a graceful service restart via the process supervisor so that the appliance can be restarted after configuration changes or when it becomes unresponsive.
acceptance_criteria: Captures uptime_before_ms from systemctl before issuing the restart
Runs systemctl restart {service_name} via SSH
Polls GET /health/ready every 2 seconds up to graceful_timeout_seconds
Returns success, service_name, restart_issued_at, came_up_at, uptime_before_ms, health_after
Returns success: false if the service does not come up within the timeout
Risk level is 'high'

generated_stories.title: session_search Tool
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want to search prior session turns by keyword so that I can recall past incident resolutions and operator instructions when handling current issues.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts query (required), limit (default 10, max 50), and optional role filter
Delegates to session-store.searchTurns()
Returns results with session_id, started_at, trigger_type, turn_index, role, excerpt, created_at
Role filter works correctly for 'user', 'assistant', and 'tool'
Risk level is 'read'

generated_stories.title: Orchestrator Post-Session Hook — Memory Update and Skill Creation
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the orchestrator to run a post-session hook after each session closes so that MEMORY.md is updated and novel incidents trigger skill creation.
acceptance_criteria: postSessionHook() is called after every session with sessionId, trigger, toolCalls, and finalText
health_check result of 'healthy' updates MEMORY.md applianceHealth section
health_check result of 'degraded' or 'unreachable' updates applianceHealth and activeAnomalies
backup_run success updates MEMORY.md lastBackup section
backup_run failure updates lastBackup and activeAnomalies
shouldCreateSkill() returns true when trigger_type != email query, tool calls >= min_tool_calls_for_skill, and status = complete
When shouldCreateSkill() is true and no matching skill exists in skills_fts, skill creation is triggered
Skill creation generates a valid agentskills.io document via claude-sonnet-4-6

generated_stories.title: Orchestrator — Context Compression Integration
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the orchestrator to check for compression need before each Claude API call so that long sessions are automatically compressed without losing context.
acceptance_criteria: contextCompressor.needsCompression(messages) is called before every client.messages.create() call
If needsCompression returns true, contextCompressor.compress(messages, sessionId) is called and the returned array replaces the live messages array
The Claude API call proceeds with the compressed messages array
A subsequent messages.length check confirms the array is shorter after compression
SessionFSM transitions to 'compressing' state during compression and back to 'running' after

generated_stories.title: SessionFSM — Session Lifecycle State Machine
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the orchestrator's session lifecycle managed by a @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm machine so that all state transitions are guarded and testable.
acceptance_criteria: SessionFSM has all 7 states: idle, running, awaiting_tool, awaiting_approval, compressing, complete, error
All key transitions from §19.1 are implemented with correct guards
Transitioning to 'awaiting_approval' for read-risk tools throws (guard violation)
Both running and awaiting_tool can transition to error on fatal_error event
compressing → running roundtrip works end-to-end
complete and error both transition to idle on reset
Invalid state transitions throw rather than silently failing

generated_stories.title: SkillCreationFSM — Skill Creation Lifecycle State Machine
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the skill creation workflow managed by a @cognitive-fab/sam-fsm machine so that the novel incident detection, search, generation, and persistence steps are guarded.
acceptance_criteria: SkillCreationFSM has all 6 states: idle, evaluating, searching, generating, validating, persisted
not_novel event from evaluating transitions to idle without calling skills_fts
match_found event from searching transitions to idle (existing skill flagged for improvement)
no_match event from searching transitions to generating
invalid event from validating transitions back to generating for up to 2 retries
retry_exceeded after 2 failed validations transitions to idle and logs failure without saving a skill
Happy path idle → persisted results in a new row in skills.db

generated_stories.title: Cron Scheduler — Full Phase 2 Schedule
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want the cron scheduler updated with all 6 Phase 2 tasks so that backup, reporting, and digest automation run on schedule without operator intervention.
acceptance_criteria: backup task fires at 0 3 * * * and sends the backup trigger message
backup_verify task fires at 5 3 * * * and sends the verify trigger message
shift_report task fires at 0 6 * * * and sends the shift report trigger message
archive_check task fires at 10 3 * * * and sends the archive check trigger message
weekly_digest task fires at 0 2 * * 1 and sends the digest trigger message
health_check remains at 0 * * * * from Phase 1
All 6 tasks are registered from appliance.yaml cron config
Weekly digest is not sent if a digest was sent within the last 6 days (168 hours)

generated_stories.title: CLI Interface — Interactive Local REPL
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want a local CLI REPL that pipes each line to the orchestrator so that COSA can be used interactively for debugging and setup.
acceptance_criteria: startCli() starts a readline REPL on stdin/stdout
Each line dispatched creates a new runSession({ type: 'cli', source: 'cli', message }) call
Response text is printed to stdout after the session completes
Typing 'exit' or pressing Ctrl+C terminates the process cleanly
CLI starts automatically when --cli flag is passed to main.js
CLI starts automatically when NODE_ENV=development and stdin is a TTY
Each CLI line is an independent session (no multi-turn state between prompts)

generated_stories.title: OPERATIONS.md Seed and Context Layer Integration
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want OPERATIONS.md seeded for WeatherStation and included as Layer 2 in the system prompt so that COSA understands normal appliance behavior from the first session.
acceptance_criteria: config/OPERATIONS.md exists with WeatherStation seed content covering normal behavior and known issues sections
contextBuilder.build() reads OPERATIONS.md and includes it as Layer 2
OPERATIONS.md is included in the cached Layers 0-4 block
OPERATIONS.md is read from disk once at session start and not re-read within the session
Phase 2 spec note honored: COSA reads OPERATIONS.md but does not write it (auto-update deferred to Phase 3)

generated_stories.title: Daily Shift Report Email Delivery
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want the cron shift_report task to format and send a plain-text email to the operator every morning so that they receive a daily summary without requesting it.
acceptance_criteria: Subject line is '[COSA] Shift Report: YYYY-MM-DD'
Email body matches the format in §11.1: period, summary block, conditions breakdown, anomalies, system status
Email is plain text — no HTML
Last backup status from MEMORY.md is included in the system status section
No email is sent if the shift_report tool returns an error (alert is sent instead)
A second shift report is not sent if the cron fires again within 6 hours (deduplication via alerts table)

generated_stories.title: Weekly Digest Email Delivery
generated_stories.description: As COSA, I want the cron weekly_digest task to compile and send a weekly operational summary email so that the operator has a regular overview without requesting it.
acceptance_criteria: Subject line is '[COSA] Weekly Digest: week of YYYY-MM-DD'
Email body matches the format in §11.2: health check summary, backup summary, anomalies, skills, operator activity
Digest gathers data via session_search calls for backup and health events
Skills section reports new skills created and skills improved this week
No second digest is sent if a digest was sent within the past 6 days
Email is plain text — no HTML

generated_stories.title: Phase 2 End-to-End Staging Tests
generated_stories.description: As a developer, I want acceptance tests T-2.1 through T-2.10 to pass against the WeatherStation mock so that Phase 2 exit criteria are verified before declaring the implementation complete.
acceptance_criteria: T-2.1: Backup automation — JSONL and .sha256 files created; backup_verify returns verified: true; MEMORY.md updated; no alert email on success
T-2.2: Shift report delivery — email arrives with correct subject and body; no second email within 6 hours
T-2.3: Skill creation from novel incident — new row in skills.db after 3+ tool call session; skill document has required sections; MEMORY.md recentIncidents updated
T-2.4: Skill reuse — skill appears in Layer 5 on second matching session; skill_uses table updated
T-2.5: Memory persistence — MEMORY.md updated after health check; contents appear in system prompt of next session
T-2.6: Context compression — array compressed at turn 13; length ≤ 8; raw turns preserved in session.db
T-2.7: Settings change approval — approval email sent; token accepted; station_info updated
T-2.8: Session search — COSA calls session_search and returns excerpts from matching prior sessions
T-2.9: Weekly digest — email sent with correct subject; no second digest within 6 days
T-2.10: CLI interface — health_check called and response printed; process exits cleanly on 'exit'
